item.title: Northern Ontario has become an unlikely film and TV production hotspot
item.description: The postNorthern Ontario has become an unlikely film and TV production hotspotappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Canada and Japan sign new strategic partnership, including joint military drills in Arctic
item.description: On final stop of 10-day trip, PM Carney announces deeper ties with Japan in defence, energy, critical minerals and advanced technology such as artificial intelligence

item.title: What country is Trump threatening with a trade embargo? Take our business and investing news quiz
item.description: Test your recollection of the top stories of the week ending March 6

item.title: Markets today: Wall Street futures slip as Middle East conflict rages on
item.description: Oil set for steepest weekly gain since Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine

item.title: Lessons for central bankers from geopolitical shocks past
item.description: Higher inflation and lower investment are the norm, with the magnitude of the geopolitical shock crucial

item.title: U.S. says bombardment to ‘surge dramatically’ as Israeli strikes on Iran, Lebanon intensify
item.description: Iran launches attacks on Gulf countries hosting U.S. forces

item.title: Anthropic’s boss apologises for bashing Pentagon—but still plans to sue
item.description: The AI lab is the first American firm to be labelled a supply-chain risk

item.title: Anthropic designated ‘supply chain risk’ by Pentagon, AI company says
item.description: Pentagon bars government contractors from using Anthropic’s technology in U.S. military work, CEO says

item.title: Iran conflict drives up fertilizer costs during busy planting season
item.description: Farmers face more disruption as Strait of Hormuz shutdown chokes shipping passage

item.title: Military power is still, unfortunately, the ultimate power
item.description: That’s a lesson Canada can learn from the new war in the Middle East

item.title: Toronto fintech linked to missing restaurant tips used restricted cash to fund operations
item.description: XTM Inc. is planning to seek a buyer or investor, company’s CEO says in creditor protection filings

item.title: Trump’s maritime security vow for Strait of Hormuz is implausible after his Venezuelan turnabout
item.description: Trump’s promise for the strait lacks credibility because of his record of reneged commitments

item.title: B.C. last-mile delivery giant UniUni raises $85-million to support expansion
item.description: RBC provides US$55-million credit line alongside US$30-million equity raise led by Chinese private equity firm

item.title: Iran strike may be the first AI war, and it won’t be the last
item.description: As artificial intelligence helps identify targets and compress battlefield decisions, there are concerns that human oversight is shrinking

item.description: The factors that affect mortgage rate pricing are all pointing up

item.description: The postThe Bank of Canada won’t commit to a launch date for open bankingappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: U.S. weekly jobless claims steady in February, layoffs fall sharply
item.description: Applications for state unemployment benefits were flat at 213,000 in the most recent week, consistent with stable labour market conditions

item.title: Formula One is attracting a different sort of fan
item.description: Lifestyle as much as motor racing is drawing in newcomers

item.title: A short guide to email opening lines
item.description: “I hope you are well” and other classics

item.title: Bayer spies an end to a long legal battle
item.description: After a settlement over Roundup, what about break-up?

item.title: Greater Toronto home sales, new listings and prices all fall in February
item.description: Regional real estate board estimates more than 100,000 buyers are holding off on making a home purchase

item.title: Inside the unravelling of seafood magnate John Risley’s billion-dollar empire
item.description: The Nova Scotia entrepreneur has hit a series of home runs with his businesses. Now he’s seeking court approval to restructure US$776-million in debt

item.title: Canadian Natural to defer oil sands mine expansion, citing uncertainty on environmental policy
item.description: Calgary-based company says it is waiting for clarity around carbon pricing and methane emissions rules

item.title: CVCA urges Ottawa to put $750M fund behind growth-stage firms
item.description: The postCVCA urges Ottawa to put $750M fund behind growth-stage firmsappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Tanker attack in the northern Persian Gulf boosts oil and gas prices, signals Iran war is widening
item.description: Marks the first time a ship was hit beyond the Strait of Hormuz area

item.title: To understand why countries grow, look at their firms
item.description: The third way in development economics

item.title: India’s economy is not as big as economists thought
item.description: But it is growing faster

item.title: Americans’ electricity bills are up. Don’t blame AI
item.description: Were it not for data centres, prices might be even higher

item.title: Markets today: Stocks end sharply lower as oil spikes to above US$80 a barrel amid intensifying Middle East fighting
item.description: Plus, markets start to price in risks of BoC rate hikes this year, dividend fund managers have a good year, and David Rosenberg on when he’ll be buying stocks

item.title: Carbon removal coalition forms with goal of attracting $100-million in project investments
item.description: Leaders in Canada’s nascent industry joined with corporate, financial backers as well as federal government

item.title: China’s rising influence over the oceans
item.description: It has been hailed as a diplomatic triumph, but some worry it could become a vehicle to increase China’s influence over the high seas

item.title: Climate tech proves strategic value in Europe’s push for autonomy
item.description: Start-ups producing green technologies play vital role in political shift towards security and independence

item.title: Iran launches missiles at Israel, U.S. bases as new Israeli strikes hit Lebanon
item.description: Fighting continues after American submarine sank an Iranian warship and Tehran threatened military and economic infrastructure across region

item.title: European pensions are a $30trn missed opportunity
item.description: If only more countries went Dutch

item.title: Fed report says U.S. economy solid but notes disruption from Minnesota immigration crackdown
item.description: U.S. economic activity rose, prices increased and employment levels stable in recent weeks, Beige Book report says

item.title: Canada’s savings rates hold steady as global tensions threaten energy markets
item.description: This week’s highest savings account and guaranteed investment certificate rates

item.title: What the heirs to General Electric did next
item.description: Their success illustrates the benefits of breaking up—and more

item.title: Rise of non-bank actors in debt markets could pose financial risks, Macklem says
item.description: Bank of Canada Governor says growing role of hedge funds, private credit in lending hasn’t been stress tested by periods of economic upheaval

item.title: U.S. private payrolls see largest increase in seven months in February, ADP data show
item.description: Private employment rose by 63,000 jobs last month, while January payrolls were revised sharply lower

item.title: When Canada and Australia team up, nobody can stop us
item.description: The two middle powers could have a powerful combined influence when working together, Scott Stirrett writes

item.title: Canada’s services economy declines further in February amid trade uncertainty
item.description: Activity in the sector has contracted for the last four months, S&P Global’s PMI data show

item.title: Maritime and ports businesses push for global shipping climate deal
item.description: Almost 90 international groups back ‘certainty’ on carbon levy opposed by US

item.title: Stamp duty is Britain’s quiet growth killer
item.description: The rate of housebuilding has plummeted over decades; to achieve recovery, the government needs to scrap high transaction taxesTax

item.title: Pro-Iran ‘hacktivists’ could target Canadian organizations, experts warn
item.description: Political activists, journalists and human rights advocates also likely to be targeted, the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security says

item.title: AGT Food and Ingredients stock stumbles on first day of trading
item.description: Agrifood giant’s shares shed 7.6 per cent value as company raises $450-million in IPO

item.title: Submarine bidders open to Ottawa splitting contract, say procurement speed setting new standard
item.description: Ottawa’s economic approach to contract could influence similar deals in the future, CEO of German submarine maker TKMS says

item.title: Fertility rates are dropping, but not among older women – and that creates a ripple effect
item.description: Today, the birth rate for 37-year-old women is near triple what it was in 1977

item.title: Why war isn’t always good for defence stocks
item.description: They win only if governments want just enough weapons—but not too many

item.title: The nightmare war scenario is becoming reality in energy markets
item.description: The longer the war in the Gulf, the harsher the global economic fallout

item.title: The Iran war is a jolt to Dubai’s business model
item.description: Can the emirate keep people and investors coming?

item.title: The push to regulate chatbots after Tumbler Ridge is raising privacy fears
item.description: The postThe push to regulate chatbots after Tumbler Ridge is raising privacy fearsappeared first onThe Logic.
